question.text: Which SEBI circular dated January 2023 allowed exchanges to launch multiple contracts on the same commodity?

answer.text: SEBI Circular dated 11/01/2023 on allowing multiple contracts on the same commodity
SEBI Circular dated 10/05/2023 on FPI direct market access
SEBI Circular on Commodity Index Options, March 2022
SEBI Circular on Forward Contract Regulation, 2015

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI Circular dated 11/01/2023 allowed different versions of the same commodity to be traded — e.g., separate contracts of 1 gram Gold, Mini Silver, Jeera mini, mini versions of Aluminium, Zinc, Lead. **The Trap:** The May 2023 circular is about FPI DMA. March 2022 was about index options. 2015 was the regulatory transfer. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers SEBI circulars related to product design in equity derivatives. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Mini contracts make commodity derivatives more accessible to retail participants — lower ticket sizes reduce the capital barrier to entry for hedging and investing.
